question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Thanks — this matches a known incident (KF11) and I can summarise what to treat this as and the first checks to run. Summary of what’s happening (matches your description) - Report Designer either errors intermittently or hangs when opening Edit in General Ledger → Financial Reports; older reports show the author as “unknown” and new/opened reports can return “Report cannot be found.” This is reported across EMEA and in both UAT and PROD, i.e. a global/multi‑environment problem

No irregularities were found in the network; ADFS can be excluded since only PROD was modified over the weekend. Next step: Data Mart Reset. No further indications of the root cause found. 02.04.24 08:28 | Admin, CRM | WEB Ticket was assigned to Employee 13. 03.04.24 10:58 | Microsoft CRM Portals, # | WEB Hi, is there an update here? question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . What category this fits (high level) - This is an infrastructure / security-layer issue affecting the Financial Reporting stack (AOS, Financial Reporting Service, Data Mart, IIS/TLS layer). The symptoms (slow Report Designer start, “unknown” authors, “report not found”) are specifically listed consequences of TLS / cipher or IIS/security misconfiguration in the reporting architecture guidance
context: SOURCE 25: ﻿ general info & description Management Reporting & Financial Reporting Infrastructure Standard Version 1.0 Date: 12.04.2024 Status: Approved This document describes the architecture, security configuration and operational standards for the Financial Reporting (Management Reporter / Financial Reports) infrastructure within the D365 Finance & Operations environment of Customer Company 11. The objective is to ensure: • Stable Report Designer startup • Access to existing reports • Consistent report metadata • High-performance report generation • TLS-compliant security configuration The reporting infrastructure depends on: • AOS servers • Financial Reporting Data Mart • IIS components • TLS / Cipher Suite configuration • Service accounts • SQL backend The system is configured consistently across environments (UAT, PROD). design Architecture overview User (Browser / Report Designer) → AOS Server (Application Object Server) → Financial Reporting Services → Data Mart (SQL) → IIS / TLS Layer Financial Reporting components 1. Financial Reporting Service Hosts Report Designer and Report Rendering Engine 2. Data Mart Provides aggregated financial data 3. AOS Server Handles authentication and communication 4. IIS Layer Responsible for TLS connections and Cipher Suites Security concept • TLS encryption according to company standard • Only defined Cipher Suites allowed • Best-practice configuration according to Microsoft recommendations • Uniform configuration across all environments Misconfigurations in TLS or Cipher Suites can result in: • Report Designer starting extremely slowly • Reports not loading • Authors appearing as “unknown” • Reports not being found Operational principles • Changes to Cipher Suites must be performed synchronously in all environments • After adjustments, a restart of the AOS servers is required • Data Mart reset is only relevant in case of data inconsistency • Global issues usually indicate infrastructure or security layer problems decision tree & use cases Initial question Does the issue occur only in one environment or globally (UAT + PROD)? A) Issue only in one environment 1. Check Data Mart reset 2. Check SQL connection 3. Check AOS service 4. Check IIS configuration B) Issue in multiple environments simultaneously → Infrastructure or security configuration likely Further checks: 1. Were Cipher Suites changed? 2. Were TLS policies adjusted? 3. Was a Windows security update installed? 4. Were IIS settings modified? C) Reports show “unknown” as author 1. Check service account connection 2. Check authentication handshake 3. Check TLS compatibility D) Report Designer extremely slow 1. Test direct access via AOS Server 2. Check network latency 3. Check TLS/Cipher negotiation E) Report not found 1. Check Data Mart consistency 2. Check report metadata 3. Check TLS communication between components

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Because it affects multiple environments and many users simultaneously, the decision tree in the architecture guidance points to an infrastructure or security configuration root cause rather than a single-user or single‑environment problem

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . Immediate checks to run (ordered) 1. Confirm whether any Cipher Suite or TLS policy changes were applied recently (servers, IIS, group policies). Misconfigured/unsynchronized cipher suites are a prime suspect and can cause exactly these symptoms

context: SOURCE 25: ﻿ general info & description Management Reporting & Financial Reporting Infrastructure Standard Version 1.0 Date: 12.04.2024 Status: Approved This document describes the architecture, security configuration and operational standards for the Financial Reporting (Management Reporter / Financial Reports) infrastructure within the D365 Finance & Operations environment of Customer Company 11. The objective is to ensure: • Stable Report Designer startup • Access to existing reports • Consistent report metadata • High-performance report generation • TLS-compliant security configuration The reporting infrastructure depends on: • AOS servers • Financial Reporting Data Mart • IIS components • TLS / Cipher Suite configuration • Service accounts • SQL backend The system is configured consistently across environments (UAT, PROD). design Architecture overview User (Browser / Report Designer) → AOS Server (Application Object Server) → Financial Reporting Services → Data Mart (SQL) → IIS / TLS Layer Financial Reporting components 1. Financial Reporting Service Hosts Report Designer and Report Rendering Engine 2. Data Mart Provides aggregated financial data 3. AOS Server Handles authentication and communication 4. IIS Layer Responsible for TLS connections and Cipher Suites Security concept • TLS encryption according to company standard • Only defined Cipher Suites allowed • Best-practice configuration according to Microsoft recommendations • Uniform configuration across all environments Misconfigurations in TLS or Cipher Suites can result in: • Report Designer starting extremely slowly • Reports not loading • Authors appearing as “unknown” • Reports not being found Operational principles • Changes to Cipher Suites must be performed synchronously in all environments • After adjustments, a restart of the AOS servers is required • Data Mart reset is only relevant in case of data inconsistency • Global issues usually indicate infrastructure or security layer problems decision tree & use cases Initial question Does the issue occur only in one environment or globally (UAT + PROD)? A) Issue only in one environment 1. Check Data Mart reset 2. Check SQL connection 3. Check AOS service 4. Check IIS configuration B) Issue in multiple environments simultaneously → Infrastructure or security configuration likely Further checks: 1. Were Cipher Suites changed? 2. Were TLS policies adjusted? 3. Was a Windows security update installed? 4. Were IIS settings modified? C) Reports show “unknown” as author 1. Check service account connection 2. Check authentication handshake 3. Check TLS compatibility D) Report Designer extremely slow 1. Test direct access via AOS Server 2. Check network latency 3. Check TLS/Cipher negotiation E) Report not found 1. Check Data Mart consistency 2. Check report metadata 3. Check TLS communication between components SOURCE 0: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF11: Management Report not working, neither in UAT nor in PROD Ticket number: TI-722511 Company: Customer Company 11 Contact person: Contact Person 7 Owner: Employee 13 Origin: Internet Type: Incident Created on: 27.03.24 14:42 Closure Closure date: 11.04.24 08:18 Closure text: Best-practice cipher suites activated with IISCRYPTO and AOS servers restarted Activities Emails: 27.03.24 15:14 | New portal note for ticket: KF11: Management Report not working, neither in UAT nor in PROD #027038962 | NO-REPLY | employee14@consultingcompany.ch A new note was submitted via the portal. SOURCE 25: ﻿ general info & description Management Reporting & Financial Reporting Infrastructure Standard Version 1.0 Date: 12.04.2024 Status: Approved This document describes the architecture, security configuration and operational standards for the Financial Reporting (Management Reporter / Financial Reports) infrastructure within the D365 Finance & Operations environment of Customer Company 11. The objective is to ensure: • Stable Report Designer startup • Access to existing reports • Consistent report metadata • High-performance report generation • TLS-compliant security configuration The reporting infrastructure depends on: • AOS servers • Financial Reporting Data Mart • IIS components • TLS / Cipher Suite configuration • Service accounts • SQL backend The system is configured consistently across environments (UAT, PROD). design Architecture overview User (Browser / Report Designer) → AOS Server (Application Object Server) → Financial Reporting Services → Data Mart (SQL) → IIS / TLS Layer Financial Reporting components 1. Financial Reporting Service Hosts Report Designer and Report Rendering Engine 2. Data Mart Provides aggregated financial data 3. AOS Server Handles authentication and communication 4. IIS Layer Responsible for TLS connections and Cipher Suites Security concept • TLS encryption according to company standard • Only defined Cipher Suites allowed • Best-practice configuration according to Microsoft recommendations • Uniform configuration across all environments Misconfigurations in TLS or Cipher Suites can result in: • Report Designer starting extremely slowly • Reports not loading • Authors appearing as “unknown” • Reports not being found Operational principles • Changes to Cipher Suites must be performed synchronously in all environments • After adjustments, a restart of the AOS servers is required • Data Mart reset is only relevant in case of data inconsistency • Global issues usually indicate infrastructure or security layer problems decision tree & use cases Initial question Does the issue occur only in one environment or globally (UAT + PROD)? A) Issue only in one environment 1. Check Data Mart reset 2. Check SQL connection 3. Check AOS service 4. Check IIS configuration B) Issue in multiple environments simultaneously → Infrastructure or security configuration likely Further checks: 1. Were Cipher Suites changed? 2. Were TLS policies adjusted? 3. Was a Windows security update installed? 4. Were IIS settings modified? C) Reports show “unknown” as author 1. Check service account connection 2. Check authentication handshake 3. Check TLS compatibility D) Report Designer extremely slow 1. Test direct access via AOS Server 2. Check network latency 3. Check TLS/Cipher negotiation E) Report not found 1. Check Data Mart consistency 2. Check report metadata 3. Check TLS communication between components
